artifacts.description: A typed change contract containing scope, claims, criteria, and definition of done.
Canonical plan.json and manifest.json generated from the contract.
An independent review attached to the proposed change.

assumptions.statement: The initial contract is accepted directly by the project owner before external review enforcement exists.
The organization currently has one human maintainer available to approve governance changes.

decisions.title: Development governance is a deterministic contract
Signed identity is enforced at the GitHub trust boundary
Product specifications are deterministic contract inputs
OverlayKit is a secure show-centered production studio
Production intents name their target and direct Program authority
Production cues compose intents with explicit partial outcomes
Control feedback separates server truth from delivery acknowledgement
Feedback admission binds signed provenance to device authority
Device credential generations bind exact authority
Device credential persistence is single-instance atomic
Device bearers use versioned domain-separated verifiers
Protocol publishes one compiled ESM implementation
Server composes device authority before network listen
Device control derives exact authority from route coordinates
Action catalogs project capability and authority intersection
Device action catalogs are authenticated point-in-time discovery
Component visibility feedback derives only from server-known structure
Apache-2.0 licensing and Rodrigo Vicente attribution are enforced distribution metadata
Device visibility feedback is issued from fresh authority with durable audience sequences
Signed device control frames bind catalog deltas to renewable server truth
Device connection authority is exclusive and retired through linearizable barriers
Device WebSocket transport authenticates before upgrade and precedes readiness
Device readiness requires current target-complete snapshot application
Device sequences require exclusive observable durable reservation
Device bootstrap payloads require atomic current signed issuance
Device connections require continuously evidenced effective authority
Device credential authority requires exclusive observable SQLite ownership
Device readiness authority requires durable transition evidence

gates.description: Typed change contracts satisfy their schema and epistemic invariants.
The deterministic governance host validates and verifies the stored plan and manifest.
A reviewer other than the last producer approves the change.
Lint, type-check, and automated tests pass.
The workflow commit and every pull-request commit carry an identity signature verified by the GitHub root of trust.

mechanisms.expectedCommand: npm run governance:observe:github -- --run artifacts/governance-run.json --out artifacts/github-evidence.json --observation-out artifacts/github-observation.json
mechanisms.subject: github-root-of-trust-evidence

rules.statement: An accepted decision is never edited or deleted; a successor decision derives supersession.
An accepted product specification is never edited or deleted; a successor specification derives supersession.

Passing pure projection tests may not be presented as HTTP publication, server inventory authority, saved-Scene discovery, Take, value, trigger or cue support, feedback, Studio parity, Companion integration, hardware behavior, or execution authorization.
The complete action inventory must be bounded, supported, canonical, and unique before projection; malformed inventory cannot yield a partial catalog.
A catalog descriptor may describe an action but must never grant command execution; every command still requires fresh authorization at its execution boundary.
Catalog descriptors must omit bearer and credential material, interactive identity, granted scope lists, production revisions, and assertions of current state.
Equivalent inventory and authority must produce byte-equivalent catalog output using fixed semantic ranks and locale-independent text ordering without mutating inputs.
An action may be projected only when its Show, required scope, exact Preview or Program target, canonical control identifier, and explicit inventory capability all intersect.
An agent identity has no independent project authority and must name its human principal.
Version-two architecture, product, security, and documentation changes reference the specifications whose observable criteria authorize their scope.
Every change separates facts, inferences, assumptions, and unknowns; facts and inferences require evidence.
Studio sessions, OBS output tokens, and device tokens are separate, revocable credentials with least-privilege scopes.
One cue operation identifier is idempotent only for identical cue content and expected initial revision.
Cue steps execute in declared order and execution stops at the first failed step.
A cue names one Show and one Preview or Program target that applies to every contained intent.
Every cue preserves ADR-0005 target revision checks, direct Program authorization, runtime-only mutation, and honest receipt scope.
One production action is a one-step cue and sequential coordinated work uses the same cue contract with additional ordered steps.

Every device action must resolve and authorize the bearer against the exact route-derived authority before Show lookup or production mutation so rotation, expiration, revocation, and scope changes fail closed on the next request.
The coordinator must recheck the monitor currency witness before queueing, within the serialized credential lane, and immediately before installing the exclusive connection slot.

Transition evidence may persist bounded identifiers, hashes, generations, targets, revisions, signer identifiers, reasons, and times but must never persist raw or sealed bearers, scopes, labels, control definitions, editorial content, or complete snapshot bytes.
A ledger sequence, hash, record, or readiness witness must never authorize admission, reconnection, a command, or production mutation and must not be exposed by the public ready notification.
Replacement, rotation, revocation, expiration and Show archival must deny later operation admission before waiting for begun work and transport closure.
A lifecycle barrier may report success only after every affected begun operation settles and every affected transport closes successfully.
A device connection must fail closed when its last positive authoritative observation reaches ten seconds or when source, resolver, clock, notification, or scheduler evidence becomes unavailable.
Expiration must schedule a close-and-drain barrier without traffic, and inability to schedule that deadline must prevent connection admission.
After host detection of changed, expired, or unavailable authority, the old connection may never regain operation authority; restoration requires a new authenticated connection.
Device authorization requires the credential's exact Show, declared scope, allowed Preview or Program target, and allowed control identifier.
Every device action and feedback-authority projection resolves the presented bearer against current expiration, revocation, generation, and sealed-secret state before admission.
Before upgrade authority can be granted, the host must arm authority observation before rereading current effective authority and must reject or invalidate every change observed across that race.
An authority notification, including one with the admitted hash, may trigger revalidation but cannot renew the positive-evidence timestamp without an authoritative reread.
Production REST and WebSocket listeners may be created only after protocol loading, device credential store initialization, and lifecycle composition succeed.

Invalidation must quiesce the exact lease immediately, reject queued and new operations, permit only already-admitted operations to settle, and close or forcibly terminate transport within twelve seconds.
Only successful issue and rotate responses may disclose a device bearer, those responses must be non-cacheable, API responses must omit sealedSecret, and persistence must omit bearer plaintext.
Issuance and rotation return an opaque bearer once while credential storage receives only its sealed representation.
Device control accepts exactly one Bearer credential from Authorization and must reject credential transport through query parameters, request bodies, cookies, or duplicate Authorization fields.

A bootstrap acknowledgement must use the supported version and type, an authorized explicit Preview or Program target, canonical lowercase SHA-256, exactly one applied or bounded error outcome, and no extra or free-text fields; its target-hash pair must have been issued on that connection.

Passing coordinator evidence may not be represented as production mounting, real frame construction or signing, durable sequence reservation, certified network delivery, command or delta authority, close-code or backpressure policy, renderer or OBS observation, Companion integration, physical hardware application, or operator perception.
One absolute twelve-second bootstrap deadline starts only after exclusive connection authority is granted and cannot be reset by target changes, retries, acknowledgements, or adapter delay.

All target snapshot attempts on one connection must use one serialized emission lane and strictly increasing positive global issuer-audience sequences; retries must capture current truth into fresh bytes and sequence.
An authorized target with no current scene or components must be represented as a valid complete revision-zero snapshot and may satisfy readiness through the ordinary current applied acknowledgement.

Snapshot currency must be checked synchronously immediately before transport, during acknowledgement admission, and before readiness grant against target revision, current durable catalog generation, immutable signer, and the three-second capture-age limit.
A target revision change invalidates only that target without consuming a retry, while a shared catalog or signing-key change invalidates all affected target evidence and requires fresh issuance.
One issuance attempt must read ProductionState exactly once and derive its complete target snapshot and effective authorized catalog observation from that same cut.

Passing issuer evidence may not be represented as production mounting, proof that all mutations are observed, automatic orphan-marker recovery, signing-key distribution or certification, permission-change lifecycle closure, certified network delivery, command or delta authority, Companion integration, renderer or OBS observation, physical device state, or operator perception.

Malformed, unauthorized, never-issued, or cross-target acknowledgements and invalid sequence, snapshot, hash, capture, construction, signing, hashing, or scheduling behavior must close fail-closed without granting readiness; only issued non-current pairs and exact duplicate current applied acknowledgements may be ignored.
A connection may become ready only after every credential-authorized target has both successful transport-send completion and an applied acknowledgement for its current snapshot hash.

A committed target-local production revision must invalidate and reissue only that target, while a shared catalog generation change must invalidate and reissue every authorized target without consuming delivery retries.

Each target permits one initial attempt plus three retries shared equally by send failure, a three-second post-send acknowledgement timeout, and current bounded error acknowledgement; exhaustion by any target closes the whole connection.

After current catalog persistence, issuance must reserve from the shared durable issuer-audience sequence lane before payload construction and signing, and every returned sequence remains consumed after any later outcome.

A target state change must invalidate that target's pending or applied pair, preserve its accumulated retries, retain other targets' evidence, coalesce intermediate changes, and schedule latest truth without charging a retry.
A device bootstrap frame must use version two, carry a positive catalog generation inside canonical payload bytes, and admit only a detached signature over those exact immutable bytes.

The inventory adapter must stop before exceeding 1,000 aggregate capabilities, bound labels to protocol limits, and allow duplicate target-control identities to fail the complete pure projection rather than silently choosing one.
Every device catalog request must freshly authenticate exactly one bearer and must not accept cookies, query authority, duplicate Authorization fields, or a Studio session as device authority.
The authenticated device authority must name the exact route-derived Show, and inventory may be read only after that Show is confirmed to exist and remain active.
Selecting a catalog entry never bypasses fresh command authorization, active-Show checks, expected revision, idempotency, or domain validation at execution time.

A CommonJS server must load the ESM catalog projector through native asynchronous import behind an injected port and must verify the compiled runtime rather than synchronously requiring the ESM package.

Unexpected catalog persistence uncertainty must block the affected audience without lowering its guarantee or reusing an old generation, while recovery may occur only after the current pending generation is successfully persisted.

One full audience credential shares a positive catalog generation across Preview and Program; every effective catalog change including ABA invalidates prior generations immediately and emission remains blocked until the current generation is durably committed.

Passing this HTTP slice may not be presented as feedback delivery, expected-revision delivery, catalog version coherence, distributed atomicity, saved-Scene discovery, Take, value, trigger or cue support, Studio parity, Companion integration, or hardware behavior.
Visibility inventory is derived from immutable copies of currently loaded Preview and Program element trees, including nested components and excluding unloaded targets.
HTTP catalog responses must be marked no-store and must not include current value, production revision, credential material, or any claim that a descriptor authorizes future execution.
A connection lease must capture immutable credential, generation audience, Show and expiration identity; a credential cannot change Show and one audience cannot change expiration.
A connection authority identity must be SHA-256 over the exact canonical effective-authority payload and must exclude the bearer, sealed verifier, label, and administrative timestamps.
At most one exact lease for a credential may admit work, and a replacement may become effective only after the prior transport closes and its begun operations drain.
A transport close reason is diagnostic lifecycle metadata and may not establish an individual control value or availability state.

Passing coordinator evidence may not be represented as WebSocket authentication or delivery, lifecycle-route integration, command execution, signed-frame delivery, close-code behavior, multi-instance coordination, cancellation of begun work, crash recovery, Companion integration, renderer or OBS observation, or hardware behavior.

Passing monitor evidence may not be represented as a production-mounted authority source, guaranteed cross-process notification delivery, physical network timing, internal operation cancellation, command or delta authority, Caddy or TLS verification, device hardware state, renderer or OBS observation, or operator perception.
A new or replaced action descriptor may become available only when its initial authoritative observation is carried in the same atomic frame.
The first frame must be bootstrap and must contain the complete bounded target catalog, no removals, and exactly one authoritative initial observation for every included action.
Each frame must bind lowercase SHA-256 over the versioned code-unit-ordered canonical bytes of its exact resulting Show and target catalog.

Passing local device HTTP tests may not be presented as proof of network delivery, renderer or OBS application, device feedback, catalogs, cues, Take, value mutation, Companion or hardware support, device-specific rate limits, audit retention, multi-process coordination, or in-flight revocation or Show-archival linearizability.
A delta builder must receive the complete current authorized target catalog and observations even though it emits only additions, replacements, removals, and changed values.

Passing frame-protocol evidence may not be represented as WebSocket authentication or delivery, commands, timers, three-second scheduling, connected or disconnected projection, operation error delivery, backpressure, reconnect implementation, server integration, key lifecycle, Companion integration, renderer or OBS observation, or hardware behavior.
Every admitted device control frame must bind its normalized complete payload to a pinned issuer, credential-generation audience, positive monotonic sequence, and verified signature.
Frame reduction must validate prior state, revision, confirmation time, transition completeness and resulting catalog hash before returning any new state.
Only a signed catalog-bound server confirmation may renew unchanged active or inactive controls; a transport acknowledgement may not renew their truth.
Device visibility execution must preserve ProductionService expected-revision, operation-id replay, operation-id conflict, Preview isolation, and bounded server-state receipt semantics.
A device Control API request must resolve only device authority and must never authenticate, construct, or inherit a Studio session principal.
A control removed from the resulting authorized catalog must become unavailable atomically rather than unknown, disconnected, or failed.

A self-hosted OverlayKit server must hold one long-lived exclusive Node 24 SQLite connection as the only executable credential authority and must not expose device control when that ownership cannot be acquired.
Issue, rotation, revocation, and effective expiration must publish immutable post-commit authority or null to current affected listeners before the corresponding Owner mutation may report success.

Legacy credential JSON may be parsed and imported only as one complete transactional migration whose exact committed input is recorded before archival; it may never remain a parallel executable authority.
A post-commit listener failure cannot roll back persisted truth and must fail only that credential audience closed by offering null to all of its current listeners.
Every successful permission change must atomically persist a new complete authority generation and bearer, making the prior token invalid at the transaction commit boundary.
Credential create and replacement must commit transactionally, and replacement may succeed only when the persisted generation equals the caller's expected generation.
SQLite credential authority may persist only validated sealed secret material and must never persist a raw OverlayKit device bearer.
Every SQLite credential row and structured authority field must satisfy the complete credential lifecycle contract before the record is exposed or mutated; malformed persisted authority fails closed.

Passing adapter tests may not be presented as primitive certification, memory or host compromise resistance, transport security, bearer recovery, replay prevention, rate limiting, endpoint integration, or hardware support.
Entropy or sealing failure prevents credential persistence, and verifier parsing, digest, or comparison failure denies authentication.
ProductionService may receive direct Program authorization from the device route only after the same request bearer has been freshly authorized for the exact Program target and control.

The validated request, credential-generation audience, credential authority, and issuer key identifier must be captured once and must not be reread from mutable collaborators after an asynchronous boundary.
Visibility feedback subjects must come only from the current authorized action catalog and observations must come only from one committed ProductionSnapshot for the exact target.
A device feedback issuer may be exposed to an adapter only after its sequence store and required public protocol ports initialize successfully with an externally supplied signer.
Every feedback issuance must freshly authenticate the device credential and require feedback-read authority for the exact Show and production target before projection, reservation, or signing.
After a sequence reservation succeeds, signing failure may create a gap but no reserved sequence may be reused.
One issuance must return the complete canonically signed ordered projection or no batch; projection, reservation, or signing failure must never return a partial batch.
An exact contiguous sequence batch must be persisted by immutable issuer key identifier and credential-generation audience before signing begins.

Passing issuer and file-store evidence may not be represented as key lifecycle, multi-process coordination, directory-fsync power-loss durability, network delivery, three-second scheduling, acknowledgement, reconnect recovery, renderer or OBS observation, Companion integration, or hardware state.
Credential lifecycle accepts Owner identity, clock, entropy, secret sealing, and storage authority only from trusted adapters outside the bearer credential.
Device credential issue, rotate, and revoke operations require an authenticated Owner session and the interactive browser-origin boundary.
Issue authority comes from an existing active Show route, and rotate or revoke must reject a stored credential that does not belong to the active Show named by that route.
Receiving or queueing an instruction grants no authority; an operation may begin only after its current exact lease is synchronously admitted at the execution boundary.
A valid device bearer resolves to device authority and never to a Studio user, role set, cookie, or interactive session.

A device connection may become ready only after current authority and complete exact-target bootstrap evidence are synchronously validated and durably appended; readiness projection and notification must follow that commit.
Rotation preserves credential identity, replaces its secret, increments generation, and binds feedback audience to credentialId.generation so prior generations fail closed.

Quiescing and closure must remove device authority before their audit append, and append uncertainty must close device admission and transports and terminate the host rather than restore or retain authority.
A production device secret must be generated from exactly 32 bytes returned by the approved cryptographic random source and encoded as canonical unpadded base64url.

A cooperating server sequence store must acquire one exclusive ownership marker for the canonical document path before loading or reserving, and a second live authority for that path must fail closed.

Passing file-store evidence may not be represented as physical-media proof, protection from non-cooperating writers, distributed authority, automatic orphan recovery, mounted operator health visibility, real frame construction or signing, WebSocket delivery, renderer or OBS observation, Companion integration, or hardware state.
Explicit close must reject newly requested reservations, drain reservations accepted before close, verify marker ownership, and remove only the closing authority's marker.

No reserved sequence may be returned, signed, or exposed to transport before the complete next document is written to a private temporary file, file-synchronized, atomically replaced, and directory-synchronized when supported.

Only a directory synchronization operation specifically reported unsupported may lower the guarantee and continue; malformed state, exhaustion, ownership conflict, file synchronization, replacement, unexpected directory synchronization, permission, and other I/O failures must fail closed without returning a reservation.

The sequence authority must report power_loss_resilient only while directory synchronization succeeds and must report process_restart_resilient whenever unsupported directory synchronization forces automatic degradation.

All device message producers using one authority must reserve from the same monotonically increasing lane keyed only by immutable issuer key and credential-generation audience; targets and message kinds may not create independent counters.
Every successfully returned sequence remains consumed forever even when later frame construction, signing, sending, or acknowledgement fails.

Passing bootstrap and Owner HTTP tests may not be presented as credential listing, response-loss recovery, concurrent archival linearizability, device request authentication, command or feedback transport, UI or Companion integration, registry provenance, multi-process coordination, in-flight revocation, rate-limit policy, audit logging, or hardware support.

Server shutdown must synchronously stop new upgrades and device admission before retiring authority, draining only admitted operations, closing credential storage, and forcibly terminating remaining transports within twelve seconds.

Passing SQLite authority evidence may not be represented as abrupt-power or physical-media durability, network-filesystem coordination, hostile database access prevention, cross-process listener delivery, proxy or TLS verification, bootstrap or command authority, Companion integration, hardware state, renderer or OBS observation, or operator perception.

Passing transition-ledger and bootstrap evidence may not be represented as cryptographic audit signing, audit-key lifecycle, hostile out-of-band database resistance, abrupt-power or physical-media durability, archive or retention verification, bootstrap-key custody, command or delta execution, macro support, proxy or TLS verification, Companion integration, hardware state, renderer or OBS observation, or operator perception.
The complete canonical transition history and its separately committed durable head must validate before a runtime may append a new host epoch or expose audited device readiness.
The device transition ledger must use the same long-lived exclusive SQLite connection as credential authority and must never open or project a second executable authority.

Every transition record must have one positive global sequence, the exact previous global hash, the exact previous connection hash when connection-scoped, bounded canonical evidence, and a SHA-256 record identity.
After verifying an unsealed prior host epoch, the next successful startup must append an explicit discontinuity and close every surviving connection chain without restoring prior authority.
The device verifier construction applies only to a 256-bit machine-generated secret and may not replace the approved KDF for human passwords.
A stored device verifier must use the accepted version and algorithm and hash the complete bearer after the device-credential domain separator.
Authentication must reject unsupported or noncanonical verifier representations before comparing an equal-length 32-byte candidate and stored digest with the approved constant-time primitive.

The device visibility route must derive Show, Preview or Program target, and canonical `<componentId>.visibility` control authority from route coordinates and must fix the required scope to component.visibility:write.
A device visibility body may contain only visible, operationId, and expectedRevision and must not name or override Show, scope, target, component, control, role, or direct-Program authority.

Passing gateway evidence may not be represented as production mounting, exact credential-failure auditing, admission rate or timeout policy, TLS or proxy certification, lifecycle or natural-disconnect retirement, snapshot or acknowledgement delivery, retries, deadlines, ready command execution, deltas, close-code policy, distributed coordination, Companion integration, renderer or OBS observation, or hardware behavior.

A device request may reach handleUpgrade only after exactly one Authorization bearer authenticates into current credential authority; invalid credential outcomes must share one public 401 shape and must not reach coordinator admission.

Coordinator admission may receive only immutable credential identifier, generation audience, generation, Show, and expiration authority captured from the authenticated credential, never a client-selected Show or Studio or Output identity.
A device WebSocket handshake must reject every Origin header and every cookie or query authority source before upgrade.
A device WebSocket handshake must select the highest mutually supported versioned device subprotocol and reject the request before upgrade when no supported version intersects.
The production WebSocket listener must route /ws only to browser and OBS transport, /device only to non-browser device transport, and reject every other path without handler fallback.
Every message received before later readiness evidence must be rejected as not_ready without parsing, buffering, queueing, or execution.
Completing 101 establishes transport only and cannot be represented as exclusive connection authority, synchronized device state, command authority, or successful device application.
A change is ready only when every enforced gate passed and every enforced artifact is present for the current planHash and manifestHash.
A governance run may report outcomes only for gates and bindings already present in its matching plan and manifest.
Credential audience and feedback scopes come from externally established authority and may never be granted by fields inside the feedback envelope.
Trusted feedback signs a canonical envelope containing its schema version, issuer key, credential audience, sequence, and complete normalized event.
An explicit operation failure remains failed until a strictly later authoritative-server observation recovers the control.
Feedback admission verifies the signature against an issuer key pinned outside the received envelope.
A server observation is current for less than three seconds and becomes stale with unknown button state at exactly three seconds.
A feedback sequence at or below the last admitted sequence is replay and cannot reach the projector.
A signed event is admitted only with feedback-read scope for its exact Show, Preview or Program target, and control identifier.
Control feedback reports authoritative server truth only and cannot be presented as renderer or hardware observation without separate evidence.
Every control feedback projection names one Show, one Preview or Program target, and one control identifier; feedback for another subject fails closed.
Hardware and third-party controller support integrates through the vendor-neutral Control API and never defines the core production command model.
Normative governance is the compiled plan derived from accepted immutable decisions, accepted immutable specifications, and the active profile.
OverlayKit source and public npm package distributions governed by this repository must declare SPDX Apache-2.0.
Each supported package root must carry the byte-exact official Apache License 2.0 text with SHA-256 cfc7749b96f63bd31c3c42b5c471bf756814053e847c10f3eb003417bc523d30.
npm run check must execute the deterministic licensing verifier and its hostile tests before the general repository quality suite.

Passing licensing controls may not be presented as proof of third-party ownership, trademark rights, contributor agreements, dependency compatibility, software-bill-of-materials completeness, registry publication, historical relicensing, legal advice, or jurisdiction-specific enforceability.
Each supported package root must carry the canonical NOTICE naming Rodrigo Vicente and linking @rodrigoteamx at https://x.com/rodrigoteamx.
One production bootstrap invocation must share one initialized device credential store and its lifecycle across all mounted Owner lifecycle routes.
Only bounded normalized fields covered by the signature may enter the trusted feedback projector; extra fields are discarded.
A component exposes operator capabilities through typed control definitions rather than inferred variables or arbitrary implementation access.
A failed cue reports every completed step, the failed step and error, and the resulting revision without claiming rollback or atomicity.
The default production workflow prepares Preview and promotes it to Program through an explicit Take command.
Every production intent names Preview or Program and a missing or unknown target fails closed.
Direct Program authority is established by an authorization boundary and is never asserted by the intent payload itself.
A CommonJS consumer that loads protocol runtime values must use native dynamic import; synchronous require and a separate CommonJS implementation are outside the accepted contract.
Every supported protocol entry point must declare explicit types and import targets, and every target must be present in the publication payload.

Passing tarball-consumer tests may not be presented as registry publication, provenance, semantic-version compatibility, browser or bundler compatibility, mixed loader identity, synchronous require support, server composition, endpoint integration, or complete downstream compatibility.
Publication-boundary evidence must exercise an installed npm tarball and may not substitute direct workspace-source imports for packed-consumer behavior.
The protocol publication allowlist must expose compiled dist artifacts rather than repository source or tests.
The public protocol runtime must be compiled from its TypeScript source as one ESM implementation accompanied by declarations.
README, package documentation, contribution terms, and landing content must communicate the current Apache-2.0 license and Rodrigo Vicente attribution without a current MIT claim.
Every public npm package allowlist and packed payload must include LICENSE and NOTICE.
A server receipt reports resulting authoritative server state and cannot be presented as renderer or device observation without additional evidence.
Preview and Program state never overwrite a saved Scene or Component Definition without an explicit save-back command.
Replaying one operation identifier with the same intent does not apply twice, and reuse for another intent fails as a conflict.
A production intent changes runtime state only and never overwrites saved Scene source.
Only a revisioned authoritative-server observation may establish current active or inactive control state.
The CommonJS server must load the ESM protocol runtime through native dynamic import, and its production build must reject a rewrite of that package boundary to synchronous require.
Every saved scene, production session, output, and show-local control belongs to an explicit Show.
Authoring, production, library, security, and integrations share one authenticated Studio shell; the headless OBS output remains separate.
A production intent validates the expected revision of its named target before mutation.
Transport acknowledgement is delivery metadata and may never establish, refresh, or recover production state.
A component visibility capability uses the canonical control identifier `<componentId>.visibility` in catalog and authorization subjects.
Each projected visibility observation must bind the snapshot Show, exact Preview or Program target, canonical catalog control subject, snapshot revision, and explicit observation time.
A supplied action catalog selects eligible subjects but the visibility projector does not establish catalog freshness, future execution authority, or coherence with later production revisions.

Opacity, visibility, classes, clipping, transforms, computed style, renderer pixels, OBS state, delivery, and hardware state may not alter or be claimed by server-known structural visibility projection.
A component may be reported active only when its own known inline display state and that of every ancestor are not display none after whitespace and case normalization.

Passing the pure projection may not be presented as trusted provenance, clock authority, signed or authenticated delivery, three-second scheduling, acknowledgement, operation failure, reconnect recovery, renderer observation, Studio or Companion consumption, or hardware behavior.

Projection must reject Show mismatch, malformed relied-upon structure, malformed descriptors, duplicate subjects or component identifiers, missing addressed components, invalid revisions or times, observation before a known snapshot update, and inputs exceeding 1,000 elements or actions without emitting partial output.
Equivalent valid inputs must produce canonically ordered equivalent observations without mutating the snapshot or catalog.
Every workspace manifest and lockfile workspace entry must declare Apache-2.0, and every workspace manifest must attribute Rodrigo Vicente with the canonical X URL.

specifications.title: Secure show authoring and live production
